sub_pixel_system.notes: No sub-pixel system data documented for this puzzle-action game

special_mechanics.name: No Jump Mechanic
special_mechanics.description: Mario cannot jump because of the hammer's weight. Movement is limited to horizontal traversal and ladder climbing on grid-based levels.

special_mechanics.name: Grid-Based Movement
special_mechanics.description: Playfield divided into invisible grid where each space contains one object. Movement is constrained to grid navigation.

special_mechanics.name: Ladder Climbing
special_mechanics.description: Player can climb destructible and indestructible ladders as part of level navigation without jumping.

tier_notes: Tier 3 - Empirical/Qualitative. Wrecking Crew '98 is a puzzle-action game where precise movement physics are not primary design concerns. Documentation focuses on gameplay mechanics (hammer-based destruction, grid-based levels, competitive multiplayer) rather than detailed physics parameters. No jump physics exist due to deliberate game design. Horizontal movement speed, acceleration, and gravity values are not documented in available sources.
